page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a virtual machine instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz and features a substantial 504 MB L3 cache, alongside 2 MB L2 and 48 KB L1d caches. This configuration includes 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, while lacking any integrated GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core scores, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance is also robust, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. The CPU features advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations, which support its strong performance in LLM inference tasks. Specifically, prompt processing for 135M, 7B, and 70B models ranks in the top 25%, while text generation for a small 135M model reaches the top 10% tier.

With a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 3.75 GB per core and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, this instance offers high resource density for data-intensive operations. It is well-suited for workloads requiring fast local storage and strong single-thread performance, such as database operations, static web serving, and localized software compilation. The absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, but its AMX-enabled CPU provides a cost-efficient alternative for smaller-scale LLM inference and text processing tasks without the overhead of dedicated accelerator hardware.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a large 504 MB L3 cache, though it lacks dedicated GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core execution, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tests ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core capabilities and memory performance consistently place in the top 25%. Supported by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, this instance delivers strong performance in LLM inference and cryptography. It is highly suited for database operations, static web serving, and text processing workloads that benefit from fast local storage and high-speed single-threaded execution.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a 32-vCPU virtual server with 120 GB RAM and 1745 GB local NVMe SSD storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with AMX and AVX-512 instruction support.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and 1745 GB local NVMe SSD, powered by an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced 32-vCPU compute instance featuring high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, advanced matrix extensions, and top-tier single-core processing performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB RAM.
Equipped with 1745 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded compression benchmarks.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 for accelerated mathematical and AI inference workloads.
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and text processing.
